advantages: Works when labeled data is expensive, incomplete, or unavailable—common in early exploration and new products.
Surfaces hidden structure (segments, manifolds, outliers) that supervised models trained only on labels may never reveal.
Enables visualization and compression of high-dimensional data for human interpretation and cheaper storage.
Learned representations can bootstrap downstream supervised tasks, reducing labeling requirements.
disadvantages: Without labels, objective success is ambiguous—clusters or embeddings may be mathematically valid but business-irrelevant.
Many methods assume specific structure (spherical clusters, linear subspaces) that real data may violate.
Hyperparameters such as cluster count k or latent dimension are hard to set without external validation.
Results can be unstable under feature scaling, sampling noise, or random initialization—requiring careful preprocessing and repeats.
